title: keyring: NoStorageAccess should map to Unavailable
description: keyring::Error::NoStorageAccess (credential store is locked or inaccessible) is mapped to SecretError::Backend in both get() and put(). This is a transient condition — the credential store may become accessible after a retry or daemon restart. It should map to SecretError::Unavailable, consistent with how other backends handle transient service unavailability. The is_no_storage() test helper also won't reliably detect this error because it string-matches for substrings that don't appear in keyring::Error::NoStorageAccess's Display output (which is 'Couldn't access platform secure storage: ...').
